description: Minimum quality thresholds for the Dynamic Inventory Reorder Agent. Evaluated in CI on every push to main. A build FAILS if any metric falls below its threshold.

thresholds.justification: Faithfulness measures whether the agent's answers are grounded in retrieved context (no hallucination). A threshold of 0.80 ensures the agent is not fabricating critical procurement data such as stock levels or supplier pricing. Setting it 10% higher (0.90) would require near-perfect RAG retrieval and unfairly penalize edge-case queries where context is sparse. Setting it 10% lower (0.70) would allow an unacceptable hallucination rate — one in three answers could contain invented facts, which is dangerous for automated purchasing decisions.

Answer relevancy measures whether the agent's response directly addresses the user's query. A threshold of 0.85 ensures the agent stays on-task for inventory management requests. A higher threshold (0.95) would fail borderline-but-useful responses that provide additional context the user didn't explicitly ask for. A lower threshold (0.75) would allow too many tangential answers to pass — the procurement manager needs precise, actionable information, not vague replies.

Tool call accuracy measures whether the agent invokes the correct tool for each query type. A threshold of 0.80 permits occasional reasonable substitutions (e.g., using query_knowledge_base instead of get_sales_data for historical questions) while preventing gross misroutes such as calling generate_purchase_order without completing analysis first. Below 0.80 would indicate systematic tool routing failures; above 0.90 would be too strict for a multi-tool agent with overlapping tool capabilities.
